steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content: The blank describes how López-Flores's conservation approach can be used with other Amazona species. The reason given is that A. vittata "corresponds in physiology, behavior, and ecology" to those species — what worked for one similar species should work for others.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
A. replicable — replicable means capable of being reproduced or repeated elsewhere. Plug in: "the conservation approach may be replicable across the genus" — because the species are physiologically similar, the same method can be applied again to other species. Correct.

B. comprehensible — comprehensible means easy to understand. Plug in: "the approach may be comprehensible across the genus" — the passage is about whether the method can be applied elsewhere, not whether it is easy to understand. Eliminated.

C. observable — observable means able to be seen or noticed. Plug in: "the approach may be observable across the genus" — observing an approach is not the same as being able to use it on other species. Eliminated.

D. redeemable — redeemable means capable of being saved or compensated for. Plug in: "the approach may be redeemable across the genus" — this implies the approach is flawed and needs rescuing, which contradicts the passage's positive framing. Eliminated.
